mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 8% signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment declines below 7% without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals strong real economic activity that drives corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Both INDPRO and GDPC1 rise but 60-day SPX returns fall below -2% in > 40% of cases, or their combined rank IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in residential construction (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns during housing market booms.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS both rise but 20-day SPX returns remain negative in > 50% of cases, or their interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly job gains (PAYEMS) reflect robust labor market conditions that boost consumer confidence and corporate revenue, producing consistent S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX underperforms by > 1.5% in 60% of months with PAYEMS growth, or the series shows no significant correlation with 5-day returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 700 thousand units signals weak demand for residential construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite declining HOUST, or housing starts recover without equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) creates uncertainty about future rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise but 20-day SPX returns remain positive in > 50% of cases, or their interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with slowing inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal reduced liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS rises above 2% with CPIAUCSL below 2% but SPX returns remain positive in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).
